summary: Matched Dolphin DVD filesystem code by replacing asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.s with src/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.c, reportedly pulling the match over from Pikmin. The PR also expanded DVD and OS boot headers needed for the C implementation, switched obj_files.mk to build the C object, and documented a real stack-size bug in lbFile_8001634C caused by the newly correct DVDFileInfo layout being 4 bytes larger due to its callback field.
what_changed: Added a 207-line C implementation at src/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.c covering __DVDFSInit, DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, DVDFastOpen, DVDClose, DVDReadAsyncPrio, and the static cbForReadAsync callback wrapper.
Updated obj_files.mk to build $(BUILD_DIR)/src/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.c.o instead of $(BUILD_DIR)/asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.s.o.
Added FSTEntry to src/dolphin/dvd/dvdfs.h with isDirAndStringOff, parentOrPosition, and nextEntryOrLength fields.

Expanded DVDFileInfo in src/dolphin/dvd/dvd.h with a callback field at offset 0x38, added DVD state/result constants, DVD_MIN_TRANSFER_SIZE, DVD_FILEINFO constants, DVD_AIS_SUCCESS, and extern OSThreadQueue __DVDThreadQueue.
Added src/dolphin/os/OSBootInfo.h with OSBootInfo fields including DVDDiskID, magic, version, memorySize, consoleType, arenaLo, arenaHi, FSTLocation at 0x38, and FSTMaxLength.

Changed lbFile_8001634C to use a local OldDVDFileInfo and casts to DVDFileInfo* for DVDFastOpen and DVDClose, preserving the old stack allocation size while documenting the out-of-bounds write caused by the real DVDFileInfo size.
smart_moves: Reused a known matching implementation from Pikmin rather than re-solving the whole Dolphin dvdfs translation from scratch.
Kept the build graph simple and verifiable by replacing only the dvdfs object path in obj_files.mk.

Modeled FST entries with compact accessor macros such as entryIsDir, stringOff, parentDir, nextDir, filePosition, and fileLength, matching the original bit-packed filesystem table behavior without overcomplicating the struct.
Made DVDFileInfo layout more accurate by adding the callback field used by DVDReadAsyncPrio and cbForReadAsync.
Handled the discovered lbFile_8001634C stack mismatch explicitly with an OldDVDFileInfo compatibility struct and a BUG comment instead of hiding the issue behind a conditional.
Preserved matching-sensitive behavior in DVDReadAsyncPrio, including panic line numbers and DVD_MIN_TRANSFER_SIZE bounds checks.
decomp_lessons: When a library struct becomes more accurate, audit existing stack locals that used the old incomplete layout; matching code may rely on the old allocation size even if the callee writes the full real struct.
Document known original-game undefined behavior directly when it is required for matching and future maintainers should not 'fix' it accidentally.
Cross-project decomp matches can be valuable for shared Dolphin SDK code, but still need local integration fixes for headers, object lists, and game-specific call sites.
For packed SDK data such as the DVD FST, small helper macros can express bitfield interpretation while keeping generated code close to the original.
Accurate callback fields matter for async APIs: DVDReadAsyncPrio stores the user callback in DVDFileInfo and the internal cbForReadAsync retrieves it through the embedded DVDCommandBlock.
naming_conventions: Kept SDK-style public symbols: __DVDFSInit, DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, DVDFastOpen, DVDClose, DVDReadAsyncPrio, __DVDThreadQueue, __DVDLongFileNameFlag.
Used lowercase static/internal helper names matching source intent, such as cbForReadAsync and isSame.
Named FST-related globals after their original roles: BootInfo, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory.
Used offset-annotated struct comments for DVDFileInfo and OldDVDFileInfo fields.

The PR uses the misspelled local names extention and extentionStart in DVDConvertPathToEntrynum, likely because those names helped preserve the matched translation rather than being polished API names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the entire dvdfs.s assembly unit to C in one object replacement, keeping symbol coverage aligned with the removed assembly.
Used OSPhysicalToCached(0) and OSBootInfo->FSTLocation in __DVDFSInit to reproduce the assembly's boot-info/FST initialization behavior.
Preserved static small-data globals corresponding to the removed .sbss symbols, including BootInfo, FstStart, FstStringStart, MaxEntryNum, currentDirectory, and __DVDLongFileNameFlag.
Used goto next_hier in DVDConvertPathToEntrynum to mirror the original control-flow shape for path traversal.
Maintained OSPanic strings and line constants, including dvdfs.c line 376 for 8.3-format path validation and lines 739/745 for out-of-file DVDReadAsync checks.
Used casts in lbFile_8001634C from OldDVDFileInfo* to DVDFileInfo* to keep the caller stack frame matching while still calling the corrected DVD API signature.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body itself notes the important discovered bug: lbFile_800163D8/lbFile_8001634C required the old DVDFileInfo struct length, so DVDFastOpen writes 4 bytes past the local stack allocation when using the real struct layout; the author chose a BUG comment rather than an #ifdef UB wrapper.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that remove OldDVDFileInfo or revisit the lbFile_8001634C DVDFileInfo stack overflow documentation.
Search for other call sites allocating DVDFileInfo before this PR's callback field was added.
Compare future Dolphin DVD SDK matches for reuse of OSBootInfo.h, FSTEntry, and DVD state/result constants.
Look for later cleanup of the OSBootInfo include guard, since the added header shows #ifndef DOLPHIN_OS_OSBOOTINFO_H_ but no visible #define in the provided diff.
